title: Speed Dial
description: Speed Dial is a floating action trigger that reveals a compact set of related actions in linear, radial, or arc layouts. Use it when the UI needs a persistent shortcut cluster without permanently dedicating space to every action.
intro: Speed Dial works best for high-value secondary actions that should stay nearby but hidden until needed. It supports linear, circle, semi-circle, and quarter-circle layouts, optional masking, idle close behavior, tooltip labels, and coordination so only one dial stays open at a time.

featureSections.title: Linear directions
featureSections.description: Linear layouts are the safest starting point because the expansion path stays obvious near screen edges and panels.

featureSections.title: Circle layout
featureSections.description: Use a full radial layout only when the trigger owns enough surrounding space and the dial is itself a focal interaction.

featureSections.title: Semi and quarter arcs
featureSections.description: Arc layouts fit side edges and corners where actions should expand inward rather than covering the trigger's surroundings evenly.

featureSections.title: Mask, idle close, and tooltips
featureSections.description: Masking, auto-close, and tooltip labels change how assertive or discoverable the control feels in dense interfaces.

featureSections.title: Coordination between dials
featureSections.description: When several floating triggers exist in one surface, coordination keeps the screen calmer by preventing multiple menus from staying open at once.

featureSections.title: Workspace composition
featureSections.description: A practical speed dial usually lives in a real work panel where it complements existing actions instead of floating on an empty board.

accessibility.title: Trigger and menu semantics
accessibility.body:
The trigger exposes menu semantics and controls a menu-like action list.

| Element | Accessibility behavior |
| --- | --- |
| trigger button | exposes `aria-haspopup`, `aria-expanded`, and `aria-controls` |
| action list | uses `role="menu"` |
| action buttons | use `role="menuitem"` with an accessible label |

accessibility.title: Keyboard and dismissal
accessibility.body:
The trigger itself follows normal button keyboard behavior. Once open, users should still be able to move through the revealed actions with normal focus navigation, and `Escape` dismisses the dial.

| Key | Action |
| --- | --- |
| `Enter` / `Space` on trigger | opens or closes the dial |
| `Tab` | moves between the trigger and visible actions |
| `Escape` | closes the open dial |
| click outside | closes the dial when `hideOnClickOutside` is enabled |

accessibility.title: Usage guidance
accessibility.body: Only put a small set of meaningful actions in a speed dial. If the actions need long labels, dense grouping, or deep hierarchy, a menu, toolbar, or command palette is usually a better fit.

apiSections.title: SpeedDialComponent - ui-speed-dial
apiSections.lead: Floating action cluster that reveals related actions in configurable layouts such as linear, circle, semi-circle, and quarter-circle.

blocks.title: Key inputs
rows: Controls the geometric layout of actions.
Controls the expansion direction for linear and arc layouts.
Distance used by radial and arc layouts.
Size of each revealed action button.
Shows a screen mask behind the open dial.
Closes the dial when users click outside.
Keeps only one dial open at a time by default.
Closes the dial after an idle timeout when provided.
Shows tooltip labels for action items.
InputSignal<{ variant?: Variant; appearance?: Appearance; size?: Size; shape?: Shape }>
Overrides button presentation for the trigger and revealed actions.

blocks.title: Model and outputs
rows: Emitted when the trigger button is clicked.
Emitted when the dial closes, including timeout and outside-click cases.

paragraphs: Each item is driven by the shared `MenuItem` model and can execute its own `action` callback.
Tooltip labels are especially useful when actions are icon-only and the surrounding surface does not already explain them.
